item.title: It's Never Too Late. Work By TheLifeAdjunct
item.description: This is @TheLifeAdjunct Work and has such a powerful message. It cannot be understated how much you should know that it isn't too late, there is help out there and you are loved.
item.dc:creator: KingofSadness

item.title: Wording folks, it matters!
item.description: As someone who grew up with a laughter if I did anything well followed by a "how did YOU do that??" a disbelief or told it must be a fluke, it really can be soul destroying... Encouragement is what is needed

item.title: Time for the unhinged XL Sunday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
her eyes are messing with me, but i like it. greetings online friends
so.. you looked cooler and that's a problem?
i assume he means live shrews and mice
hey that's my favorite animal, the snow leopard
my dog could not do that, no training at all
how do they know i'm not a farmer?
i love Owen's Wilson
so conceited
T2 is my favorite movie (non-comedy)
had no sound, still enjoyed
we've all done this at least once
i knew it was ok
mmm hmm
time for goblin mode
petting zoos smell
Stevie does this also
yes
that's my time.. have a great night, gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Teach Love
item.description: FP Edit: If you are reading this give some love to @titatitacapotita with some words you have needed in the past. @titatitacapotita I don't know you but I love you. If people can hate for no reason I can love. If you want a taco or a pizza. Message me. I'm not joking. You are not alone. Teach Love
item.dc:creator: ThePocho361

item.title: 15th favorite. Still one this kind of thing.
item.description: Apparently I've hit most viral so that's cool. Be cool to one another. Kindness is brave, cruelty is cowardice. It is possible to make no mistakes and still lose, that is not failure, that is life. Send cool sci-fi shit, and if you're a nerd feel free to message me for a nerdy chat! ...especially if you're a cute, single, nerd girl.....
item.dc:creator: KillerGlitch

item.title: The —requested— happy ending
item.dc:creator: blonderengel

item.title: Wholesome SUNDAY dump of smile pancakes
item.description:
1. Another good boy, must protect food man from vacuum invasion
2. You skied on the wrong trail mf
3. Corgy butt day
4. Buddies
5
6. Sunday be like
7. Gotta go fast
8. Just father and son goofing around
9
10. Those rabbits are richer than 99% of people reading this. Aghh, who am I kidding. Probably a 100%. Are rich people even on Imgur?
11. Playful boah
12. Mmmmm
13. Mmmm pt.2
14. May you have a great week and great success in everything you do : )
15. Thanks for scrolling all the way trough! Hope this dump made you happy! Finally I would like to share with you a little passion project of mine. Me and my brother are making a game that is inspired by "The Stanley Parable" and the narrator definitely has a touch a of GladOS to him. The game is part of Next Fest 2024 and it would the mean the world to us if you wishlisted: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. I sincerely hope that Keanu knows how much the internet loves him!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: I thought cigarettes would be the thing that killed me.
item.description: I had no will power to quit. Every attempt failed. I thought it was because I was weak, or the addition was too strong. My doctor offered me a medication to help me quit. I only half heartedly accepted the prescription. The medication worked by making me feel like I had smoked too much and became stronger over time. Slowly, I was smoking less and less. I finally saw an exit for the addiction and took it. I was tired of being a slave to it. I have heard that the medication has since been taken off the market. That motivates me not to have a relapse. (originally posted by @PutItInNeutral on 2023-02-26 22:55:16)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

Like what we do and how we do it? Leave a tip. Or don't. Cool, either way. Stay True & Stay Tuned - Meta Pathos

item.title: Where now the horse and the rider?
item.description:
King Théoden paraphrased a traditional hymn in the 2002 movie adaptation; this is a look at how the poem, sometimes called the Lament for the Rohirrim, went in full and what it is about.
In year 2510 of the Third Age, five centuries before The Lord of the Rings, rode a host from the North to aid Gondor in time of war. The host was led by King Eorl the Young upon his steed Felaróf, ancestor of Shadowfax, and in reward was Eorl's folk given the land hence known as Rohan (art by aegeri).
Long after, sometime between 2957 and the 2970s, came Aragorn to Rohan. He lived there for an unknown period under the alias of Thorongil, and "rode in the host of the Rohirrim" in service of King Thengel (The Return of the King; Appendix A; I; V, art by Magali Villeneuve).
In year 3019 came Gandalf the White with Aragorn, Gimli and Legolas to the capital of Rohan. Outside the city were nine burial mounds. Aragorn told his companions that in the mounds slept the sires of King Théoden, son of Thengel: "Then he began to chant softly in a slow tongue unknown to the Elf and Dwarf; yet they listened, for there was a strong music in it." – TTT; The King of the Golden Hall onwards (art by Artby_Romain)
"`That, I guess, is the language of the Rohirrim,' said Legolas; 'for it is like to this land itself; rich and rolling in part, and else hard and stern as the mountains. But I cannot guess what it means, save that it is laden with the sadness of Mortal Men.'" Aragorn translated the lament for his companions into the Common Speech [that Tolkien presented as English], as near as he could make it;
"Where now the horse and the rider? Where is the horn that was blowing? Where is the helm and the hauberk, and the bright hair flowing? Where is the hand on the harpstring, and the red fire glowing? Where is the spring and the harvest and the tall corn growing?
They have passed like rain on the mountain, like a wind in the meadow; The days have gone down in the West behind the hills into shadow. Who shall gather the smoke of the dead wood burning, Or behold the flowing years from the Sea returning?"
Aragorn finished, saying (art by Wouter Florusse): "Thus spoke a forgotten poet long ago in Rohan, recalling how tall and fair was Eorl the Young, who rode down out of the North; and there were wings upon the feet of his steed, Felaróf, father of horses. So men still sing in the evening.' With these words the travellers passed the silent mounds."
Rohan's native tongue, Rohanese, Tolkien translated using Old English, and the lament is inspired by an Old English poem from the late 10th century named The Wanderer. Notably it includes "Hwær cwom mearg? Hwær cwom mago?" meaning "Where is the horse gone? Where the warrior?" The Wanderer also contains the OE word "þeodnes" ["prince/king/lord"]; in the form Theoden it was used to translate Tûrac ["King"], Theoden's name in Rohanese (The History of Middle-earth; II; The Languages at the end of the Third Age; §54).
Some related accounts: Rohan's kings https://imgur.com/gallery/Cvbh5Y7 Aragorn https://imgur.com/gallery/DwkYgrM — — — — — In case you enjoyed this and would like to read further Tolkien lore posts about characters, themes, objects and events, here are two indexes: Character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF Folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL
item.dc:creator: Ngugi
